title: tapeinidium amboynense
intro: Tapeinidium amboynense is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to Indonesia and is also known as the 'wild asparagus' due to its edible stems. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. The stems of Tapeinidium amboynense can be prepared as a vegetable or used in traditional herbal remedies, often in the form of a decoction or infusion, to provide various health benefits such as reducing blood pressure and improving digestion.
